title: crinum lane-poolei
intro: Crinum lane-poolei, also known as the Red Lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, native to the tropical regions of Western Australia and the Northern Territory. The Red Lily has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat snake bites, fever, and skin conditions. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help to reduce pain, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the Red Lily is often used to make tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are applied topically or taken orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
